_meta.purpose: Terminology + spelling correction reference for the formatter. Load 'corrections' as a fast lookup map (case-insensitive whole-word match). Pass 'house_style', 'protected_patterns', and 'abbreviations' into the system prompt so the model standardizes consistently. GROW THIS FILE: every time a radiologist fixes something the AI got wrong, add the pair to 'corrections'. That is how the system becomes a 'master' over time — not by retraining the model, but by enriching this lexicon.
_meta.grounding: Misspelling rules verified against Radiopaedia 'Common medical misspellings' (rID 69125) and standard radiology orthography. -paenia/-coele/radio-opaque/septae/diverticulae rules are confirmed errors, NOT British variants.

spelling_style.note: Pick ONE house style and enforce it. Mixing US and UK spelling in one report looks unprofessional. Switch 'active' to 'UK' to flip. The model must convert ALL eligible words to the active style.

corrections._comment: key = wrong/variant form (lowercase), value = correct form in house style. Apply as whole-word, case-insensitive replacement; preserve the original capitalization pattern of the matched word.

protected_patterns._comment: These categories must be copied VERBATIM from the source. The model must NEVER 'correct', round, normalize, or infer them. If one looks wrong, FLAG it — do not change it.
protected_patterns.measurements: Any number with a unit: mm, cm, ml, cc, HU, mm/s, cm/s, x (as in 3.2 x 2.1 cm), %, weeks, days. Keep exact value, exact unit, exact decimal places.
protected_patterns.laterality: left, right, bilateral, midline — never swap or 'fix' these.
protected_patterns.negation: no, not, without, absent, free of, negative for, denies — a dropped or added negation reverses the medical meaning. Preserve exactly.
protected_patterns.phase_and_sequence: T1W, T2W, FLAIR, DWI, ADC, STIR, GRE, post-contrast, non-contrast, arterial/venous/delayed phase — copy verbatim.
protected_patterns.grading_scores: BI-RADS, TI-RADS, Bosniak, Grade I/II/III, ASA — copy verbatim.
protected_patterns.named_levels: Vertebral levels (L4-L5, C5-C6), segments, anatomical level labels — copy verbatim.
protected_patterns.dates_ids_names: Patient identifiers, dates, accession numbers — never alter (and ideally de-identify before sending).

ambiguous_pairs._comment: Word pairs that are both real terms with OPPOSITE or very different meanings. The model must NEVER auto-pick between them; if the source is unclear, FLAG for the radiologist.
ambiguous_pairs.note: The last two pairs are classic speech-recognition traps ('renal bone' for 'renal stone', 'new' for 'no'). Treat any occurrence in a clinically odd context as a FLAG, not a silent fix.

abbreviations._comment: Expand on first use OR keep standard short forms — set policy in system prompt. These are the accepted standard forms.

style_rules: Use complete sentences in FINDINGS; each anatomical region on its own line or sentence.
IMPRESSION is a concise numbered summary of positive/clinically significant findings only — never restate every normal.
Keep tense consistent (present tense for current findings: 'The liver is normal').
Capitalize section headers consistently (e.g., all caps: CLINICAL HISTORY, TECHNIQUE, FINDINGS, IMPRESSION).
One space after periods; no double spaces; no trailing spaces.
Numbers: use digits for measurements (3.2 cm), spell out small counts in prose where natural.
Do not use ALL CAPS for emphasis inside findings.
Avoid hedging clutter; keep each statement crisp.
Preserve the radiologist's clinical judgment wording — standardize form, not meaning.
